author: Niche Gamer
text:
Pocketpair says Palworld 1.0 is its most ambitious release ever It’s easy to forget that despite its enormous success, Palworld is still technically an early access game. That will soon change with version 1.0, and according to Pocketpair, this won’t be just another content patch. The studio is describing it as the biggest and most ambitious update in the game’s history, one that could fundamentally […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Monsters are Coming! gets Xbox release this summer Publisher Raw Fury and developer Ludogram have announced an Xbox release for Monsters are Coming!, their roguelite strategy shooter game. Monsters are Coming! is coming to Xbox Series X|S later this summer on August 6th via the Microsoft Store, as well as Xbox Game Pass. The game has been available for Windows PC (Steam) since November […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Sega explains why Sonic classic games aren’t coming back anytime soon Why are so many classic Sonic games still trapped on older hardware? It’s a fair question, but according to Sonic Team head Takashi Iizuka, re-releasing them isn’t nearly as straightforward as fans might think. Speaking during Sonic’s 35th anniversary celebrations, Iizuka explained that simply porting older titles to modern systems often isn’t enough anymore. Expectations […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Over 20,000 fans are asking Microsoft to drop Xbox’s online paywall Not long after launching their new Xbox Player Voice platform, one of the most popular requests on the platform is to make online multiplayer completely free. The petition has already attracted more than 20,000 votes and thousands of comments, making it one of the most supported ideas on the feedback portal. The argument is simple: […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Mortal Shell II Preview Mortal Shell II has opened its beta to everyone, and I have to admit, this is exactly how you showcase a game and win people over. As someone who never played the first Mortal Shell, I went into this completely blind. After spending several hours with the beta on Steam, I came away genuinely impressed. […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Pixel multiplayer roguelike game Another Door announced Publisher No More Robots and developer Mizar and Alcor Interactive announced Another Door, a stylish pixelated multiplayer turn-based roguelike game. Another Door is in development for Windows PC (Steam) with a release set for sometime later this year. A playable demo is available now, too. Here’s a rundown on the game, plus its reveal trailer: Travel […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Demon slaying shooter Armatus launches this winter Publisher Fictions and developer Counterplay Games announced a release window for Armatus, their new third-person roguelite shooter. Armatus is launching across Windows PC (Steam, Microsoft Store), Xbox Series X|S, Switch 2, and PS5 sometime this winter. Here’s a rundown on the previously announced game, plus a new trailer: ARMATUS is a third-person roguelite shooter set in the demon-infested […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Terrinoth: Heroes of Descent now available for PC and consoles Publisher New Tales and developer Artefacts Studio have released Terrinoth: Heroes of Descent, their new game set in the fantasy universe of Descent. Terrinoth: Heroes of Descent is out now globally for Windows PC (via Steam), Xbox Series X|S, and PS5. Here’s a rundown on the previously announced game, plus a new trailer: The legendary board […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Fungalpunk RPG Signet City announced Publisher Fellow Traveller and Citizen Sleeper developer Jump Over the Age have announced Signet City, a first-person “fungalpunk” RPG. Signet City is in development for Windows PC (Steam, GOG, Humble Store), though a release date wasn’t announced. Here’s a rundown on the game, plus its reveal trailer: From the creator of Citizen Sleeper, SIGNET CITY is a […]

Source

author: Niche Gamer
text:
Mr. Magpie’s Harmless Card Game gets new publishers Indie developer Giant Light Studios has announced they’ve entered an agreement for publishers on Mr. Magpie’s Harmless Card Game. Mr. Magpie’s Harmless Card Game will be published by Behaviour Interactive and Gambit Digital when it launches for Windows PC (Steam). A release date isn’t confirmed. Here’s a rundown on the game, plus a new trailer: Mr Magpie’s […]
